episode_text:
day=8; meal=general tso's chicken and broccoli; setting=Social with person on Right; time_of_day=evening;
preferences: microwave_time=2 min; occlusion_relevance=minimize right occlusion; robot_speed=medium; skewering_axis=perpendicular to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=no; transfer_mode=outside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=medium; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=button; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=button; retract_between_bites=yes; bite_dipping_preference=do not dip; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=100 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF food is a cold-served item such as fruit or dessert AND any dining context AND any affective state THEN no microwave BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected no microwave for fruit-based cold meals, preserving intended serving temperature. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special temperature constraint THEN 2 min BECAUSE this remains the best-supported conservative default for typical warm meals. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food is a typical warm entree THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user selected standard reheating in that social context, reinforcing 2 min as the stable warm-meal choice. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food is a typical warm entree THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user selected standard reheating for a hot meal in that media context. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the left AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food is a typical warm entree THEN 1 min BECAUSE the user selected lighter reheating for General Tso's chicken and broccoli while working, suggesting some work meals favor a shorter warm-up. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the right AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food is a typical warm entree THEN 3 min BECAUSE the user selected the hottest reheating level for chicken nuggets and broccoli while working with the laptop on the right, showing that work-context temperature preference is not uniform and can shift by meal/context combination. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the left AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food is a cold fruit meal THEN no microwave BECAUSE the user kept the cold-serving preference even in a work context, indicating food temperature intent overrides context.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes a person on the right OR a TV/device on the right AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose to avoid blocking salient right-side visual or social targets, now across both social and device-based right-side contexts. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user selected front-occlusion minimization when the salient visual target was directly ahead. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the user did not request left-side occlusion minimization in that media context. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the left AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food is a typical warm entree THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the user did not request left-side occlusion minimization while working. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the left AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food is a cold fruit meal THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the user again declined occlusion-based adjustment, strengthening the pattern that left-side task engagement does not by itself trigger occlusion concerns. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE no evidence yet shows occlusion avoidance is needed without a salient social or media target.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food is a typical warm entree THEN medium BECAUSE the user selected medium speed in that social setting, reinforcing medium as a comfortable socially appropriate pace. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food is a light cold item with no special handling constraint THEN fast BECAUSE the user selected faster motion in a media-attentive context, consistent with preferring a shorter, less interruptive meal. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the left AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food is a typical warm entree THEN fast BECAUSE the user selected fast speed while working, indicating that some task-focused meals prioritize efficiency. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the left AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food is a cold fruit meal THEN medium BECAUSE the user selected medium speed for fruit while working, suggesting the work-context preference for speed is moderated by meal type. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the right AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food is a typical warm entree THEN medium BECAUSE the user selected medium speed in that right-side work context, showing that work does not uniformly imply fast motion. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food is a typical warm entree THEN medium BECAUSE the user selected medium speed in that context, indicating that side media engagement does not always imply a desire for faster motion. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN medium BECAUSE this remains the conservative default absent broader evidence.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user selected the less intrusive transfer style in that social setting, consistent with maintaining comfort and explicit user participation around another person. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user selected a more robot-complete transfer style in a right-side TV context, consistent with reducing the user's need to lean forward while attending to the screen. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user again chose inside-mouth transfer in a front-TV context, indicating a broader tendency to prefer direct delivery when visually engaged with media. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user also selected inside-mouth transfer in that media context, strengthening the pattern that TV viewing generally shifts preference toward direct delivery. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the left AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food is a typical warm entree THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user selected inside-mouth transfer while working, extending the pattern that visually or cognitively engaging tasks favor direct delivery. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the left AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food is a cold fruit meal THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user again selected direct delivery while working, showing that the work-context preference is stable across food types. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the right AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food is a typical warm entree THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user also selected direct delivery in that right-side work context, strengthening the pattern that active device engagement favors inside-mouth transfer. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE this remains the conservative default and is physically feasible for the user.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer mode is outside mouth transfer AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN medium BECAUSE the user selected a moderate stopping distance that balances comfort and reachability, including in a right-side social meal. IF transfer mode is inside mouth transfer AND any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN not applicable BECAUSE this parameter only applies to outside-mouth transfer.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN LED BECAUSE the user selected a quiet visual cue in that social setting, consistent with avoiding disruptive speech while still preserving explicit signaling. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN no cue BECAUSE the user selected no cue in a front-TV context, consistent with preferring minimal interruption while attending to media. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the left AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food is a typical warm entree THEN no cue BECAUSE the user selected no cue while working on a warm entree, indicating a preference to suppress readiness notifications during some task-focused meals. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the left AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food is a cold fruit meal THEN speech BECAUSE the user selected speech in the same work context for fruit, showing that cue preference can shift with meal type rather than being fixed by context alone. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the right AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food is a typical warm entree THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user selected the most explicit multimodal cue in that right-side work context, suggesting that when the salient task target is on the same side as the robot the user may want stronger notifications. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN LED BECAUSE the user selected LED rather than suppressing cues, indicating that not all side-oriented media contexts call for no cue. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN LED BECAUSE this remains the conservative low-burden default outside observed task-focused suppression or stronger multimodal cueing.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN button BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose button-based initiation in social meals and can comfortably press buttons, consistent with avoiding ambiguity from open-mouth signals during conversation. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN button BECAUSE the user selected explicit manual readiness signaling, indicating a preference for deliberate control in that media context. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN button BECAUSE the user again selected manual readiness signaling, suggesting that some side-TV contexts favor explicit control. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected automatic initiation in a front-TV context, indicating that when attention is directed to media straight ahead the user may prefer lower-effort, less interactive control. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the left AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food is a typical warm entree THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected automatic initiation while working, suggesting task-focused contexts can shift preference from explicit control toward reduced interaction burden. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the left AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food is a cold fruit meal THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user selected open-mouth initiation for feeding in that work-and-fruit context, showing that for feeding the user may accept low-effort perceptual initiation rather than button press when the meal is light and attention remains on work. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the right AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food is a typical warm entree THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user selected open-mouth initiation in that right-side work context, indicating that active work can also favor low-effort perceptual initiation rather than button use.

IF dining context is social AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN button BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred manual readiness signaling for drinking in social settings and can comfortably press buttons. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN button BECAUSE the user selected explicit manual readiness signaling, suggesting a preference for deliberate control in that context. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN button BECAUSE the user again selected manual readiness signaling, reinforcing a side-TV preference for explicit control. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected automatic initiation in a front-TV context, consistent with preferring reduced interaction burden while focused on media. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the left AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the earlier work-context evidence favored lower-effort interaction, but this is mixed with a button selection for drinking in a fruit work meal. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the left AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food is a cold fruit meal THEN button BECAUSE the user selected explicit manual readiness for drinking in that specific work-and-fruit context, indicating more deliberate control for sip timing than for feeding. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the right AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food is a typical warm entree THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user selected open-mouth initiation for drinking in that right-side work context, showing that work can also shift drinking initiation toward low-effort perceptual signaling.

IF dining context is social AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN button BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred manual readiness signaling for wiping in social settings and can comfortably press buttons. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN button BECAUSE the user selected explicit manual readiness signaling, suggesting a preference for deliberate control in that context. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN button BECAUSE the user again selected manual readiness signaling, reinforcing a side-TV preference for explicit control. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected automatic initiation in a front-TV context, consistent with preferring reduced interaction burden while focused on media. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the left AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food is a typical warm entree THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected automatic initiation while working, extending the pattern that task-focused contexts can favor lower-effort interaction. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the left AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food is a cold fruit meal THEN button BECAUSE the user selected explicit manual readiness for wiping in that specific work-and-fruit context, indicating more deliberate control for wiping timing than the earlier work default suggested. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the right AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food is a typical warm entree THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user selected open-mouth initiation in that right-side work context, showing that work can also shift wiping initiation toward low-effort perceptual signaling.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN LED BECAUSE the user selected a quiet visual completion cue in that social setting, consistent with avoiding disruptive speech while preserving explicit timing information. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN no cue BECAUSE the user selected no cue when the salient target was directly ahead, consistent with minimizing interruptions during media engagement. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the left AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food is a typical warm entree THEN no cue BECAUSE the user selected no cue while working, indicating a preference to suppress completion notifications during some attention-demanding meals. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the left AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food is a cold fruit meal THEN speech BECAUSE the user selected speech in that work-and-fruit context, showing that completion cue preference can vary with meal type rather than being uniformly suppressed while working. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the right AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food is a typical warm entree THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user selected multimodal completion cueing in that right-side work context, suggesting stronger notification is preferred when attention is directed to a right-side device. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN LED BECAUSE the user selected LED in that media context, indicating that cue suppression is specific to some task-focused situations rather than universal. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN LED BECAUSE this remains the conservative low-burden default outside observed task-focused suppression or stronger multimodal cueing.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN button BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose button-based completion for drinking in social meals, favoring explicit control over automatic inference. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN button BECAUSE the user selected button-based completion, indicating a stable preference for explicit completion signaling in that context. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN button BECAUSE the user again selected button-based completion, reinforcing a side-TV preference for explicit control. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the left AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food is a typical warm entree THEN button BECAUSE the user kept explicit button-based completion while working, suggesting that even when initiation is automated the user prefers deliberate completion control for drinking. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the left AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food is a cold fruit meal THEN button BECAUSE the user again selected button-based completion, strengthening the pattern that drinking completion remains explicitly user-controlled in left-side work contexts. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected automatic completion in a front-TV context, indicating a lower-effort interaction style when attention is directed to media. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the right AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food is a typical warm entree THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected automatic completion in that right-side work context, showing that work can also shift drinking completion toward lower-effort automation.

IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN button BECAUSE the user selected button-based completion, indicating a stable preference for explicit completion signaling in that context. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN button BECAUSE the user again selected button-based completion, reinforcing a side-TV preference for explicit control. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the left AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food is a typical warm entree THEN button BECAUSE the user kept explicit button-based completion while working, suggesting that even when initiation is automated the user prefers deliberate completion control for wiping. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected automatic completion in a front-TV context, indicating a lower-effort interaction style when attention is directed to media. retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes a person on the right OR a TV/device on the right AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN yes BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose to reduce prolonged obstruction in right-side socially or visually relevant contexts, even at the cost of longer meal time, now including both social and device-based right-side contexts. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN no BECAUSE the user did not choose retraction despite caring about front occlusion, suggesting a preference to keep the meal moving and rely on other strategies rather than adding extra retract motions. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN no BECAUSE the user also skipped retraction in that context, indicating that retraction is not a general response to all media contexts. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the left AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food is a typical warm entree THEN no BECAUSE the user skipped retraction while working on that meal, reinforcing a general preference to avoid extra time costs in many work contexts. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the left AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food is a cold fruit meal THEN yes BECAUSE the user selected retraction in that specific work-and-fruit context, showing that when working the user may sometimes trade efficiency for reduced obstruction depending on the meal.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context is social AND affective state is unspecified/neutral THEN less BECAUSE the user selected reduced dipping for a social meal with sauce, consistent with limiting messiness. IF food is a typical warm entree without user-selected dipping behavior AND dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective state is unspecified/neutral THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user selected no dipping for a social warm entree, showing that social context alone does not create a dipping preference when the meal is not treated as a dipping target. IF food is a cold dessert or fruit with topping rather than a dippable sauce AND any dining context AND any affective state THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user selected no dipping for strawberries with whipped cream and again for a fruit plate, indicating these foods were not treated as dipping targets. IF food is a typical sauced entree without user-selected dipping behavior AND dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective state is unspecified/neutral THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user did not request dipping for the meal, suggesting sauce already on the food does not imply a desire for extra dipping. IF food is a typical sauced entree without user-selected dipping behavior AND dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the left AND affective state is unspecified/neutral THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user again declined dipping for a sauced entree, reinforcing that sauce present in the dish does not by itself create a dipping preference. IF food is a typical entree served with a dip AND dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the right AND affective state is unspecified/neutral THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user selected no dipping for chicken nuggets with ketchup, showing that even clearly dippable foods are not necessarily preferred for dipping in work contexts. IF food has no dippable item or sauce AND any dining context AND any affective state THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is not relevant.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user selected the middle wait time in a social meal, consistent with allowing time for explicit user control without making automation excessively delayed. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user selected a very long wait in a media-attentive context, consistent with wanting ample time before the robot proceeds automatically. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user again selected a very long wait, suggesting side-TV contexts generally call for more time before automatic progression. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user selected a very short wait together with broad use of autocontinue, indicating a preference for rapid, low-interaction progression while focused on media ahead. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the left AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food is a typical warm entree THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user selected a very short wait while working, extending the pattern that task-focused contexts can favor quick automatic progression. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the left AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food is a cold fruit meal THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user selected a very long wait in that work-and-fruit context, showing that food/context combinations can reverse the earlier work-context tendency toward short waits. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the right AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food is a typical warm entree THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user selected a very short wait in that right-side work context, reinforcing that many active work meals favor quick automatic progression. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective state is unspecified/neutral AND food has no special constraint THEN 100 sec BECAUSE this remains the conservative default absent broader evidence.
